patient_text: A 54-year-old man with a history of chronic alcohol use presents with severe epigastric pain radiating to the back, nausea, and vomiting. Labs reveal markedly elevated serum lipase and amylase.
criterion_text: Undergoing endoscopic treatment of pancreatic fluid collections

patient_text: A 54-year-old man with a history of chronic alcohol use presents with severe epigastric pain radiating to the back, nausea, and vomiting. Labs reveal markedly elevated serum lipase and amylase.
criterion_text: Age ≥ 18 years

patient_text: A 54-year-old man with a history of chronic alcohol use presents with severe epigastric pain radiating to the back, nausea, and vomiting. Labs reveal markedly elevated serum lipase and amylase.
criterion_text: Functional hyposplenism or asplenia, patient under long-term antibiotic prophylaxis for this reason

patient_text: A 54-year-old man with a history of chronic alcohol use presents with severe epigastric pain radiating to the back, nausea, and vomiting. Labs reveal markedly elevated serum lipase and amylase.
criterion_text: Choledocholithiasis not resolved by ERCP, post-ERCP perforation, and post-ERCP concomitant pancreatitis

patient_text: A 29-year-old woman presents with intermittent palpitations, heat intolerance, and unintentional weight loss. Physical exam reveals a fine tremor, tachycardia, and a diffusely enlarged, non-tender thyroid gland.
criterion_text: Providing signed informed consent

patient_text: A 29-year-old woman presents with intermittent palpitations, heat intolerance, and unintentional weight loss. Physical exam reveals a fine tremor, tachycardia, and a diffusely enlarged, non-tender thyroid gland.
criterion_text: Non-pregnant

patient_text: A 29-year-old woman presents with intermittent palpitations, heat intolerance, and unintentional weight loss. Physical exam reveals a fine tremor, tachycardia, and a diffusely enlarged, non-tender thyroid gland.
criterion_text: Blood routine: the total number of white blood cells < 3.0×10^9 or neutrophils < 1.5× 10^9

patient_text: A 29-year-old woman presents with intermittent palpitations, heat intolerance, and unintentional weight loss. Physical exam reveals a fine tremor, tachycardia, and a diffusely enlarged, non-tender thyroid gland.
criterion_text: Ill-controlled hypertension: diastolic blood pressure≥100mmHg, systolic blood pressure ≥160 mmHg

patient_text: A 7-year-old boy with a 3-week history of periorbital edema and frothy urine. Urinalysis shows heavy proteinuria without hematuria, and serum albumin is low.
criterion_text: Calcineurin inhibitor-resistant after ≥6 months of adequate dosing, OR calcineurin inhibitor-intolerant with documented contraindications, OR inadequate response or disease relapse after treatment with ≥2 other immunosuppressive agents

patient_text: A 7-year-old boy with a 3-week history of periorbital edema and frothy urine. Urinalysis shows heavy proteinuria without hematuria, and serum albumin is low.
criterion_text: Persistent urinary protein-to-creatinine (UP/C) ratio >1.0 g/g

patient_text: A 7-year-old boy with a 3-week history of periorbital edema and frothy urine. Urinalysis shows heavy proteinuria without hematuria, and serum albumin is low.
criterion_text: Steroid-dependence or frequently relapsing disease, unless complete remission achieved

patient_text: A 7-year-old boy with a 3-week history of periorbital edema and frothy urine. Urinalysis shows heavy proteinuria without hematuria, and serum albumin is low.
criterion_text: eGFR <60 mL/min/1.73 m² using the modified Bedside Schwartz formula

patient_text: A 68-year-old man with a long smoking history presents with painless gross hematuria. CT urography reveals a mass in the bladder wall.
criterion_text: Age ≥ 18 years

patient_text: A 68-year-old man with a long smoking history presents with painless gross hematuria. CT urography reveals a mass in the bladder wall.
criterion_text: Muscle invasive bladder cancer that is cisplatin-ineligible or declined based on Galsky criteria

patient_text: A 68-year-old man with a long smoking history presents with painless gross hematuria. CT urography reveals a mass in the bladder wall.
criterion_text: Age under 18 years old

patient_text: A 68-year-old man with a long smoking history presents with painless gross hematuria. CT urography reveals a mass in the bladder wall.
criterion_text: Histology of pure adenocarcinoma, pure squamous cell carcinoma, or pure small cell carcinoma in the TURBT sample

patient_text: A 34-year-old woman presents with recurrent episodes of severe unilateral throbbing headache preceded by visual scotomata, accompanied by photophobia, nausea, and phonophobia.
criterion_text: Documented medical history of migraine with or without aura for at least 6 months before the Screening Visit

patient_text: A 34-year-old woman presents with recurrent episodes of severe unilateral throbbing headache preceded by visual scotomata, accompanied by photophobia, nausea, and phonophobia.
criterion_text: Age ≥18

patient_text: A 34-year-old woman presents with recurrent episodes of severe unilateral throbbing headache preceded by visual scotomata, accompanied by photophobia, nausea, and phonophobia.
criterion_text: Continuous migraine (defined as an unrelenting headache) within 1 month prior to Screening Visit

patient_text: A 34-year-old woman presents with recurrent episodes of severe unilateral throbbing headache preceded by visual scotomata, accompanied by photophobia, nausea, and phonophobia.
criterion_text: Significant cardiovascular disease (unstable angina, severe arrhythmias, recent MI)

patient_text: A 45-year-old man with poorly controlled type 2 diabetes presents with fever, facial pain, black necrotic eschar on the nasal mucosa, and periorbital swelling.
criterion_text: Guardian and subject (if of independent informed age) willing and able to provide written informed consent to participate in the study

patient_text: A 45-year-old man with poorly controlled type 2 diabetes presents with fever, facial pain, black necrotic eschar on the nasal mucosa, and periorbital swelling.
criterion_text: Patients who intend to take isavuconazole for the treatment of invasive mycosis

patient_text: A 45-year-old man with poorly controlled type 2 diabetes presents with fever, facial pain, black necrotic eschar on the nasal mucosa, and periorbital swelling.
criterion_text: History of radiotherapy within 12 months prior to enrollment

patient_text: A 45-year-old man with poorly controlled type 2 diabetes presents with fever, facial pain, black necrotic eschar on the nasal mucosa, and periorbital swelling.
criterion_text: No diagnosis of probable or proven invasive pulmonary infection with Mucorales according to the 2020 EORTC/MSGERC criteria

patient_text: A 3-month-old infant with projectile non-bilious vomiting after feeding, visible peristalsis, and a palpable olive-shaped mass in the epigastrium. Labs show hypochloremic, hypokalemic metabolic alkalosis.
criterion_text: Male and female children

patient_text: A 3-month-old infant with projectile non-bilious vomiting after feeding, visible peristalsis, and a palpable olive-shaped mass in the epigastrium. Labs show hypochloremic, hypokalemic metabolic alkalosis.
criterion_text: Minor or adult patient ≥ 4 years of age

patient_text: A 3-month-old infant with projectile non-bilious vomiting after feeding, visible peristalsis, and a palpable olive-shaped mass in the epigastrium. Labs show hypochloremic, hypokalemic metabolic alkalosis.
criterion_text: Oncologic patients at risk of neoplastic cachexia

patient_text: A 3-month-old infant with projectile non-bilious vomiting after feeding, visible peristalsis, and a palpable olive-shaped mass in the epigastrium. Labs show hypochloremic, hypokalemic metabolic alkalosis.
criterion_text: lack of informed consent

patient_text: A 60-year-old woman presents with progressive dyspnea, dry cough, and clubbing of the fingers. Chest CT reveals bilateral basal reticular opacities with honeycombing.
criterion_text: Patient able to understand and sign a written informed consent form, or written informed consent form signed by the patients' legally authorized representative

patient_text: A 60-year-old woman presents with progressive dyspnea, dry cough, and clubbing of the fingers. Chest CT reveals bilateral basal reticular opacities with honeycombing.
criterion_text: Diagnosis of RA using the 2010 American College of Rheumatology (ACR) criteria

patient_text: A 60-year-old woman presents with progressive dyspnea, dry cough, and clubbing of the fingers. Chest CT reveals bilateral basal reticular opacities with honeycombing.
criterion_text: Active hepatitis B (positive hepatitis B surface antigen and HBV DNA >upper limit of normal) or active hepatitis C (positive hepatitis C antibody and HCV RNA >upper limit of normal)

patient_text: A 60-year-old woman presents with progressive dyspnea, dry cough, and clubbing of the fingers. Chest CT reveals bilateral basal reticular opacities with honeycombing.
criterion_text: Inability to give informed consent

patient_text: A 19-year-old male presents with fever, sore throat, fatigue, and posterior cervical lymphadenopathy. Physical exam reveals splenomegaly, and a monospot test is positive.
criterion_text: Age 18 years and above

patient_text: A 19-year-old male presents with fever, sore throat, fatigue, and posterior cervical lymphadenopathy. Physical exam reveals splenomegaly, and a monospot test is positive.
criterion_text: Detection of elevated levels of EBV DNA, RNA or proteins in affected tissues OR elevated levels of EBV DNA in the peripheral blood

patient_text: A 19-year-old male presents with fever, sore throat, fatigue, and posterior cervical lymphadenopathy. Physical exam reveals splenomegaly, and a monospot test is positive.
criterion_text: Any other condition that, in the opinion of the investigator, would make the patient unsuitable for enrollment or could interfere with the patient participating in the study

patient_text: A 19-year-old male presents with fever, sore throat, fatigue, and posterior cervical lymphadenopathy. Physical exam reveals splenomegaly, and a monospot test is positive.
criterion_text: Treatment with immunosuppressive medication including ≥10mg prednisone equivalent

patient_text: A 73-year-old man with sudden onset of a painless, curtain-like loss of vision in the right eye, preceded by flashes of light and floaters.
criterion_text: Present to the hospital with a visual acuity of hand motion or better in the study eye

patient_text: A 73-year-old man with sudden onset of a painless, curtain-like loss of vision in the right eye, preceded by flashes of light and floaters.
criterion_text: Planned combined phacovitrectomy procedure at one of the selected surgical sites

patient_text: A 73-year-old man with sudden onset of a painless, curtain-like loss of vision in the right eye, preceded by flashes of light and floaters.
criterion_text: Scleral buckling is indicated

patient_text: A 73-year-old man with sudden onset of a painless, curtain-like loss of vision in the right eye, preceded by flashes of light and floaters.
criterion_text: History of uncontrolled hypertension
